steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a transition word question. The first sentence says the field is "divided between dispersalists and vicariancists." The blank begins a sentence that names those two camps and explains what each believes. The correct transition must signal that the second sentence is clarifying or defining the division just mentioned.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Furthermore,’: Signals additional information on top of what was said. The sentence doesn't add a new point — it explains the same point (the division). Using "furthermore" implies the two sentences are separate ideas. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘By contrast,’: Signals opposition between the two sentences. The second sentence does not contrast with the first; it elaborates on it. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Similarly,’: Signals resemblance to a prior point. There is no parallel idea for the second sentence to mirror; it clarifies the first sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘That is,’: Signals a restatement or clarification of the preceding idea — exactly what the second sentence does by spelling out what "divided between dispersalists and vicariancists" means. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
